content.text:
Translate this to Spanish and keep the same format (Homebrewrey, markdown): 






{{frontCover}}


# Dis: Ironthrone Mountain
## Expanding Chains of Asmodeus (12th level)
___

{{banner Christopher Hamwell}}

{{footnote
 *The iron must flow . . .*
}}

![background image](https://i.imgur.com/VL02ICR.png){position:absolute,bottom:0,left:0,height:100%}

\page



{{pageNumber,auto}}




## Dis:Ironthrone Mountain

 
{{quote

*The iron must flow . . .*


}}



Read the following and paraphrase to your players as background:




{{descriptive
An imposing landscape unfolds before you. The mountains, composed entirely of jagged iron, rise into the smoky skies, their silhouettes a testament to the infernal craftsmanship that dominates this realm. This is not a natural world but a forge of damnation, where the very earth has been twisted to serve the dark whims of its ruler, Dispater.

The air is thick, not just with the oppressive heat but with the acrid scent of burning metal. The further you travel, the more the landscape asserts its malevolence, with mirages that dance along the horizon, blurring the line between reality and illusion.

Drawing closer to one of the many iron mines, the sounds of industry and suffering reach your ears, growing louder with each step. The cacophony is relentless: the metallic clang of pickaxes striking iron, the earth’s groans as it is forced to yield its infernal bounty, and the distant roar of furnaces feasting on ore. These are the symphonies of Dis, compositions of toil and torment, played out by the damned souls condemned to labor eternally within these pits.

The entrance to the mine presents itself as a gaping wound in the mountain's side. Venturing within, you are greeted by the sight of narrow tunnels illuminated by the flickering light of soul-fire torches. These torches cast shadows along the crudely excavated walls, creating an ever-shifting maze of darkness and light.

The air inside the mine is heavy, not just with physical exhaustion but with the weight of lost hope, as the souls condemned to this fate labor on, their chains clinking in a morose rhythm that punctuates the silence of surrender. Here, time loses meaning. The relentless cycle of work and brief rest periods under the watchful eyes of demonic overseers blur into a monotonous eternity. 


Despite the pervasive sense of hopelessness, there is a peculiar beauty to the hellish landscape that surrounds you. The glow of molten iron running in rivulets along the tunnel floors casts an otherworldly light, while sparks from the forges soar upwards, briefly illuminating the darkness before fading into nothingness.

}}



::::::

{{descriptive
This is Dis, where every strike of the hammer and every cart of ore extracted is a testament to the indomitable will of its infernal ruler. It is a place of paradoxes: of strength found in moments of weakness, of camaraderie forged in the depths of isolation and of beauty glimpsed within the heart of darkness.
}}




::

### Background
:
This mini-campaign unfolds in and around the ominous backdrop of Ironthrone Mountain, a colossal volcano with molten rivers cascading down its slopes (refer to the map below for adventure locations). 

Treacherous paths meander up and down the mountain, offering challenging routes for those lacking wings or magical transportation. This mountain is just one of several where infernal iron is extracted and transformed into ingots, destined for the construction of war machines, soul coins and the creation of weaponry wielded by an array of devilish entities. 
:
These adventures are designed for four characters of 12th level. 
::




### Adventure hooks
:
There could be several reasons for your heroes to journey to Ironthrone Mountain. Consider the following options:

1. **Imprisonment.** If your heroes find themselves captured in another corner of the Nine Hells, instead of facing certain death, they might be spared and sent to the mines as slaves. Consider Blackrock Hole as your players' destination.
:
2. **Rescue Mission.** Your heroes may undertake a perilous journey to the mountain with the goal of rescuing an NPC, being held against their will, or the recovery of a phylactery. In this case, your heroes' quarry will likely be found at Skulltop.
:
3. **Vengeance Against Sas.** Ironthrone Mountain is ruled by a merciless erinyes devil named Sas. Your heroes might hold a deep grudge against her, or they could be offered a lucrative contract to eliminate her. Again, Skulltop is the place to visit.
:
4. **The Mines.** This region is dotted with numerous iron mines, where enslaved individuals labor and perish with little regard for their lives. While these mines are not elaborated upon in this document, they present a viable opportunity for expansion.









\page







{{pageNumber,auto}}

### Ironthrone Mountain

::






![](https://i.imgur.com/OCKuHTd.png) {width:678px,mix-blend-mode:multiply}














\page
{{pageNumber,auto}}







The adventure locations and encounters outlined here are flexible and can be incorporated into your campaign as desired, allowing you to enrich and extend your own narrative by integrating them wherever you see fit. 
:
Regardless of the motivation behind their journey to the mountain, your players should understand one fundamental rule: *The iron must flow . . .*



::
## Adventure Locations


:

### The Ring of Fire
:
If the heroes decide to approach the mountain, they must traverse the relatively open expanse known as the Ring of Fire. 

This area, while passable without magical protection against fire, is exceedingly hot and oppressive. It is dotted with patrols of imps and spine devils on the lookout for escaped slaves, lemures shirking their duties, or any threat to the mountain's security. 



{{descriptive
As you venture closer to the heart of the infernal landscape, the ominous silhouette of the mountain looms, casting its shadow over the treacherous expanse known as the Ring of Fire. The air grows hotter, thick with the scent of sulfur and ash, making each breath a laborious task. The ground beneath your feet is scarred by the relentless flow of lava streams.

The terrain is perilous, marked by sinkholes that appear without warning, their gaping maws swallowing anything or anyone unfortunate enough to tread too closely. Spouts of lava erupt sporadically from the earth, sending magma skyward, only to rain down like fiery hailstones. The heat is oppressive, sapping your strength  with every step.

Amidst this hellish tableau, devils, the guardians of this accursed place, patrol the skies with keen eyes and malevolent intent. These winged sentinels are ever-watchful, seeking out escaped slaves, lemures attempting to shirk their infernal duties, or any perceived threats to their dominion. The presence of these demonic patrols adds to the already formidable challenge of navigating the Ring of Fire. 

The journey through the Ring of Fire is not just a physical challenge but a trial of will and courage.

}}
:



:::::::::




#### Ring Encounters
:
##### 1. Infernal Sinkhole
As the heroes cautiously navigate the Ring of Fire, the ground beneath them betrays their presence with a sudden, treacherous shift. 

Read or paraphrase the following:


{{descriptive
As you venture across the Ring of Fire, the ground beneath you suddenly betrays your presence. A massive sinkhole, over 300 feet in diameter, quickly forms. You see rocks and black sand rushing towards the gaping maw as the earth collapses into itself. The ominous glow of lava, visible far below, casts an eerie light on the scene, signaling the deadly depths that lie beneath. The air around you thickens with ash and sulfur, making each breath a heavy, cloying effort.

}}





Give the leading two heroes the opportunity to make a Perception check with a difficulty class (DC) of 18 to detect the unstable ground. 

If the heroes fail to notice the treacherous terrain, they must each make a DC 16 Acrobatics or Athletics check to evade the pull of the collapsing ground and the ominous draw of the sinkhole. Success means a desperate scramble to safety, as they manage to find purchase on the more stable ground surrounding the chasm. However, failure spells a dire fate; those unable to escape the sinkhole's clutches are sucked into its depths as it closes above them, dragging them down 30 feet on the first turn and an additional 10 feet with each subsequent turn.

Those caught in the collapsing sinkhole suffer 3d8 fire damage and 3d8 bludgeoning damage per turn as they are battered by rocks and scorched by the intense heat emanating from below. The darkness envelops them, rendering them blinded and incapacitated. Trapped within the earth's grip, they find themselves unable to speak or breathe, their situation growing increasingly grim with each passing moment.

For those unlucky enough to be drawn into the sinkhole, survival hinges on a sliver of hope: the possibility of a magical means of escape. Without it, their prospects are bleak, as the relentless damage and suffocating darkness promise nothing but a grim end.



\page



### The Ring of Fire

{{pageNumber,auto}}

{{imageMaskEdge3,--offset:-36%,--rotation:-12
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/sdj3Jre.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->





\page

{{pageNumber,auto}}

### Infernal Sinkhole

{{imageMaskEdge3,--offset:-36%,--rotation:-12
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/WXtLcUI.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->



\page




{{pageNumber,auto}}

### Basalt Bridge




{{imageMaskEdge3,--offset:-36%,--rotation:-12
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/wrNn0PF.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->









\page

{{pageNumber,auto}}



##### 2. Basalt Bridge
Read or paraphrase the following when your heroes approach the bridge:



{{descriptive
You come upon a bridge of ancient basalt that spans a river of churning lava. This structure appears almost natural in its formation, as if it were crafted by the very forces that shaped the land. 

Upon closer inspection, the eerie glow of the lava below illuminates fiendish runes carved into the stone. The air is thick with heat and the sound of the molten river roars in your ears, creating a backdrop of primal fury to this scene of dark majesty. 
}}



The bridge is 500 feet long and drops 300 feet to lava below. Fiendish runes are carved into its entire length. For those who speak Infernal or possess magical means of deciphering script, the heroes must meticulously examine the whole bridge to fully understand this sacred poem.



{{note
*Beneath the peaks where shadows dwell,*::
*In realms where silence whispers tales,*::
*There flows the blood of mountains fell,*::
*Infernal iron, through hellish vales.*::

:
*Dispater reigns, his will decrees,*::
*From Dis, his throne of dark desires,*::
*With iron born of cursed seas,*::
*He forges war, as fate requires.*::
:
*This metal, wrought in flame and fear,*::
*Becomes the sinew of our war,*::
*Machines of death, drawing near,*::
*And Soul Coins that open Stygian shore.*::
:
*Each piece a testament to pain,*::
*A pact of power, dark and deep,*::
*In Dispater's eternal reign,*::
*Where secrets and silent sorrows sleep.*::
:
*Beware, ye souls with greedy eyes,*::
*Who covet iron from these lands,*::
*For only death beneath these skies*::
*Awaits those who with theft lay plans.*::
:
*This poem, scribed in fire's light,*::
*A warning to the bold and brash,*::
*Dispater's might, through darkest night,*::
*Shall crush the fools who dare to clash.*::
}}

:::




##### 3. Infernal Wagons

Consider running this encounter if your heroes are crossing the basalt bridge, as described above.
Read or paraphrase the following:

:
{{descriptive
As you navigate the barren landscape, your path intersects with a chilling procession. You see a trio of prison wagons rattling along an ash-covered road, their frames wrought from the dark, gleaming metal of infernal iron. Each wagon is pulled by a nightmare, flames licking their hooves and casting an eerie light on the surroundings. Driving these ghastly carriages are animated suits of armor, infernal helmed horrors, their movements precise and devoid of life.

At the forefront of this macabre convoy, atop the lead wagon, you notice a commanding figure: a male cambion, his features marked by the unmistakable lineage of the infernal realms. He surveys the landscape with a cold, calculating gaze, clearly the leader of this troupe.

Within the confines of the first wagon, you glimpse three figures with fiery hair and beards, unmistakably azer, their expressions a mix of defiance and resignation. The second wagon holds a quartet of duergar, dark dwarves, their sturdy forms huddled together, eyes darting suspiciously at their surroundings. And in the third, a solitary figure catches your eye, standing out from the rest: a dwarf, whose noble demeanor persists despite captivity. 
}}





The following NPCs are present here:

###### Antagonists:
:
- Vhalok, a male **cambion**. Tasked with the grim duty of delivering prisoners to the mountain, where they will labor in the mines as slaves. 

- 3 **helmed horrors.** Under Vhalok's control, these horrors drive the carriages and obey his commands faultlessly. They will fight the heroes even if the cambion is incapacitated or killed. 

- 3 **nightmares.** The nightmares have no particular loyalty to Vhalok and will likely flee if he is slain.
:



###### Prisoners:
:
- 3 **azer** (Thranar, Magdor and Keldran). The azer were captured in Avernus seeking precious gemstones and metals. They are masters of metalworking and are highly prized slaves. The azer have no equipment. If released, the azer will gladly join the heroes if they are allowed to keep half of all precious gemstones and rare metals found. Otherwise they will part ways, perhaps to meet again.

- 4 **duergar.** These duergar crossed their previous master and stole infernal ingots from the mountain. They have no love for the heroes or their captor, and will act in their own best interests. They do not have any equipment.




\page



{{pageNumber,auto}}

### Infernal Wagons



{{imageMaskEdge3,--offset:-36%,--rotation:-12
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/i596gLK.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->





\page



{{pageNumber,auto}}

### Thranar


{{imageMaskCorner13,--offsetX:0%,--offsetY:0%,--rotation:0
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/154G1hk.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offsetX to shift the mask left or right (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --offsetY to shift the mask up or down
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->







\page



{{pageNumber,auto}}











- Bronze Stoutfoot, a dwarven **gladiator.** He is a renowned smith and metalworker, hailing from Neverwinter. Bronze was part of an adventuring company that was captured on Avernus, the dwarf being the sole survivor. He was purchased by Dispater to oversee one of the mines. The dwarf will gladly join the heroes should they rescue him and offer their companionship.



::


##### 4. Hell Worm

The heroes encounter a **hell worm** as it navigates through molten lava or tunnels just below the scorched surface of the Ring of Fire's landscape. Should the heroes be airborne, they soar above this magnificent beast, witnessing its movement from a unique vantage point. 

The hell worm is a fearsome predator, its body sheathed in scales that glimmer with a heat mirage. It is immune to fire, and its digestive system combines both acid and fire to dissolve its prey. 

Read or paraphrase the following:

:

{{descriptive
The ground trembles beneath your feet, a low rumble echoing across the blasted valley. Suddenly, you catch sight of something massive moving in the distance.

You see a colossal worm emerge from a sea of fire, its long, thick body twisting with a grace that belies its monstrous size. Its scales, a tapestry of crimson and black, shimmer in the dim light, reflecting the infernal flames that surround it. The creature's massive jaws are wide open, revealing rows of razor-sharp teeth, each one gleaming with a deadly promise. 

Its eyes fix on you, and for a moment, the world seems to hold its breath . . .
}}


:


{{monster,frame
## Hell Worm (new monster)
*Gargantuan monstrosity, unaligned*
___
**Armor Class** :: 18 (natural armor)
**Hit Points**  :: 247(15d20 + 90)
**Speed**       :: 50 ft., burrow 30 ft., swim (lava) 40 ft.
___
|  STR  |  DEX  |  CON  |  INT  |  WIS  |  CHA  |
|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|
|28 (+9)|7 (-2)|22 (+6)|1 (-5)|8 (-1)|4 (-3)|
___
**Saving Throws** :: Con +11, Wis +4
**Damage Immunities** :: Fire
**Senses**               :: 30 ft., Tremorsense 60 ft., passive Perception 9
**Languages**            :: -
**Challenge**            :: 15 (13,000 XP)
___
***Tunneler.*** The hell worm can burrow through solid rock at half its burrow speed and leaves a 10-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.


___
### Actions
***Bite.*** *Melee Weapon Attack:* +14 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. *Hit:* 22 (3d8 + 9) piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it must succeed on a DC 19 Dexterity saving throw or be swallowed by the worm. A swallowed creature is blinded and restrained, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the worm, and it takes 21 (6d6) damage at the start of each of the worm's turns due to digestive juices that deal half acid and half fire damage. If the worm takes 30 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, the worm must succeed on a DC 21 Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, which fall prone in a space within 10 feet of the worm. If the hell worm dies, a swallowed creature is no longer restrained and can escape from the corpse using 15 feet of movement, exiting prone.
:
***Stinger*** *Melee Weapon Attack:* +14 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. *Hit:* 19 (3d6 + 9) piercing damage, and the target must make a DC 19 Constitution saving throw, taking 42 (12d6) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
}}







{{imageMaskEdge1,--offset:23%,--rotation:-4
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/5zMUdQP.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->




\page


{{pageNumber,auto}}





### Hell Worm



{{imageMaskEdge3,--offset:-36%,--rotation:-12
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/XCpCXlq.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->






\page

{{pageNumber,auto}}



### Imp Scouts





{{imageMaskEdge3,--offset:-36%,--rotation:-12
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/LcLArvo.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->











\page

{{pageNumber,auto}}


##### 5. Scouts
A patrol of **imps** or **spined devils** scout the area above where the heroes are located. 

Read or paraphrase the following, altering as necessary:

{{descriptive
Your gaze is inadvertently drawn upwards by the harsh cry of the wind. Far overhead, at least 150 feet in the sky, you see a flying patrol of spined devils, small fiends whose silhouettes cut ominously against the bleak skyline. These creatures, known for their watchful nature and keen eyes, glide with a purposeful grace, their spined bodies casting fleeting shadows upon the ground where you stand.
}}



The heroes need to pass a collective Stealth check, pitted against the imps' passive Perception of 11 or the spine devils' passive Perception of 12, to stay hidden as they traverse the ring. 

If the fiends spot the heroes, they immediately split up, darting away in different directions to elude any attempt at pursuit. Shortly thereafter, they reconvene at the mountain to relay what they have observed. 

Following this report, the inhabitants within the Ring of Fire and the mountain are put on high alert, rendering them impossible to surprise through ordinary means.

Moreover, a **horned devil** is sent forth from the mountain to engage the intruders, accompanied by the imps or spined devils (whichever is appropriate). 


::











### Infernal Watchblocks
:
These two guard towers, built from basalt stone, sit heavily upon the infernal landscape like festering sores. They guard the southern entrance to the mountain, though additional towers can be positioned wherever deemed necessary. 

Garrisons of the watchblocks vary but might include the following:


- A **half-red dragon veteran,** 2 **hell hounds** and a **horned devil**.::
- 4 **spined devils** led by a **bone devil**.:: 
- 2 **chain devils** and a **helmed horror**.::

:



The watchblock depicted in the map below is constructed using a specific type of magic-infused basalt. This unique construction allows the walls to be transparent when viewed from the interior and it enables fiends to move freely through the walls. As a result of this magical property, there are no windows or openings in the structure. 
:
**Note.** Ranged attacks and magic cannot penetrate the walls, only fiends themselves and what they might be carrying. 



:









#### Area Descriptions
:
##### 1. Ground Level



The entrance to the watchblock is a massive, three-foot-thick solid basalt stone door without obvious hinges or handles. When a creature comes within 10 feet of the door from the outside, a magical mouth materializes on the door's surface, taking the form of a sinister green fiend's face. The image then utters the words, *"Speak His name and enter."*

The access code is *"Dispater"* but it must be spoken in Infernal. When the specified conditions are met, the stone slab smoothly retracts into the ground for a duration of 1 minute before closing again. 

However, if the wrong words are uttered, the visage on the door responds by shaking its head and emitting a piercing scream. This alert not only notifies the tower's occupants but also releases a cone of poisonous breath, mirroring the breath weapon of an adult green dragon (a 60-foot cone). Those in the vicinity must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or suffer 16d6 poison damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. The breath weapon regenerates at midnight. 

From the inside, the door can be effortlessly opened by the touch of any creature.













Read or paraphrase the following:

:


{{descriptive
You enter a chamber where the air is heavy with the scent of burning oil. Your eyes are immediately drawn to the four slender pillars of polished obsidian that stand sentinel in the room, each one etched with fiendish runes and serpentine shapes that seem to slither under the flickering light. On either side, alcoves cradle brass bowls filled with oil, their flames casting shadows and emitting a thick, acrid smoke.

Before you, dominating the space, is a statue that commands both fear and awe. Towering ten feet tall, the figure of Dispater is carved with such meticulous detail that it seems lifelike. Draped in crimson robes that appear to flow like liquid, the statue’s humanoid form is distinguished by its unsettling features—goat hooves stand in place of feet, sharp horns rise from its head and a dangerously spiked tail is coiled casually by its side. In one hand, the statue grips the fabled Rod of Dispater, a fearsome emblem of his unyielding dominion.

At the rear of the chamber, a wide stair curves upwards.
}}




\page

{{pageNumber,auto}}




### Infernal Watchblock

{{imageMaskEdge3,--offset:-36%,--rotation:-12
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/XSuCP3P.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->














\page

{{pageNumber,auto}}




A concealed compartment has been cunningly cut into the base of the statue, which can be discovered with a DC 20 Investigation check. The compartment is trapped with 3 needles, each coated with wyvern poison, with a DC 18 Investigation check to discover. If activated, each needle does 1 piercing damage and 7d6 poison damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.
:
The container holds the following:

- 10 vials of *truth serum* (DMG, page 258)::
- 4 Soul Coins::
- A *Ring of Telekinesis*:: 


::



##### 2. First Level

Read or paraphrase the following:



{{descriptive
This room is elegantly furnished with items that exude opulence: a foldable cot draped in scarlet silk, cabinets and wardrobes crafted from gleaming mahogany, and a modestly sized writing desk. The south walls are embellished with tapestries in shades of purple and red, illustrating the epic conflict between fiends and demons. A petite table is set with a silver platter and pitcher, adding a touch of refinement to the space. 

A staircase of black iron curls upwards.
}}





This chamber serves as the private quarters of Wyrmrakis, a **half-red dragon veteran.** Typically, Wyrmrakis is accompanied by two **hell hounds.** 

Known for his intelligence and malevolence, Wyrmrakis constantly seeks prisoners for interrogation.

Should the heroes trigger any traps on the lower level, Wyrmrakis will be forewarned and will seek to ambush them. 
:
Within this chamber, notable items of value are: 
 
- A foldable cot, valued at 50 gold pieces.

- Silverware priced at 75 gold pieces.
- A crimson *Cloak of Protection*, worn by Wyrmrakis to achieve an AC of 19, adorned with golden threads depicting the effigy of Dispater. 
**Note.** The cloak also gives Wyrmrakis a +1 bonus to all saving throws.::


::

##### 3. Grim Storage
This floor houses five chests made of black metal, each seem to be securely locked. A staircase continues to spiral upward from here.

Contrary to appearances, the chests are neither locked nor trapped, and they house the following contents:

- Gruesome rolls of flayed demon skin, now decaying and foul-smelling.
- Jars containing the dried fingers and ears of prisoners.
- A chest brimming with fine sand, within which lies the decomposing head of a hezrou demon.
- 20 vials filled with water that remains consumable, alongside dried provisions sufficient to sustain one humanoid for several weeks.
- A grimoire bound in demon hide, all of its pages torn out but one. The remaining page describes a spell named *Sinister Snare*. If you are familiar with *Asmodeus' Corrupted Grimoire*, this is a dark version of the spell *Web.*


::



#### Web: "Sinister Snare"  
*Conjuration 2nd-level*  

**Casting Time:** :: 1 action  
**Range:** :: 60 feet  
**Components:** :: V, S, M (a live spider, which must be consumed when the spell is cast)  
**Duration:** :: Concentration, up to 1 hour  
:
You summon a mass of blood-red, animate webs at a designated point within range. These webs spread across a 20-foot cube, pulsating with a malevolent energy. Unlike ordinary webs, these are imbued with a fiendish vitality, actively seeking to entangle not just the limbs but also the faces of those caught, obstructing their breathing.  

Any creature that steps into or starts its turn in the webbed area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or become restrained. A creature that fails this save by 5 or more finds its mouth and nostrils covered by the web, rendered unable to breathe until it escapes or the web is removed.  

The essence of *Sinister Snare* lies in its ability to instill fear through suffocation, a slow and terrifying fate. Its conjured webs are not merely traps but predators, reflecting the dark intent of the caster. This spell serves as a deadly obstruction.  

The spell is otherwise as described in the *Player’s Handbook*, page 285.  


::


##### 4. Guest Quarters
This level is designated as residential quarters for visiting devils and significant NPCs. 

They feature desks and tables crafted from premium hardwood along with luxurious, cushioned couches nestled in the corners. The south wall is adorned with an 8 foot tall mirror known as the *Mirror of Eldritch Echoes.*

Although these quarters are presently vacant, you are encouraged to personalize this space with your own creative additions.

#### Mirror of Eldritch Echoes
*Wondrous item, very rare*
:
This grand mirror stands at an imposing height of 8 feet, encased in a frame carved with Eldritch symbols that seem to writhe in the corner of one's eye. The glass is unnaturally clear, reflecting images with eerie perfection. 

Those who gaze into the *Mirror of Eldritch Echoes* see not their current selves but an unsettling vision of their form ravaged by the passage of time, appearing aged and withered.
:

**Eldritch Communication.** When attuned to its sister mirror, a creature that speaks a designated command word can use this mirror to see through its counterpart, allowing for visual and auditory communication across any distance, even across different planes of existence. A mirror may be able to communicate via several others.

:

**Visions of the Aged.** Once per day, a creature gazing into the mirror may seek insight into their own future. They see a cryptic vision related to their query, manifesting as a scene from their potential future. The vision is subject to the DM's discretion. Utilizing the mirror to glimpse into the future ages the user by one year.





::

##### 5. Temple to Dispater
Each watchblock has a small temple dedicated to Dispater. Read or paraphrase the following if the heroes enter this place: 




{{descriptive
You stand in a dimly lit chamber devoted to Dispater, ruler of Dis. At its center looms a black marble statue, its intricate details casting eerie shadows in the flickering candlelight. Thick incense smoke hangs in the air, heavy and intoxicating. Before the statue, a bronze bowl rests beside a sharp, curved sacrificial blade, its purpose unmistakable.

The room exudes an eerie ambiance and you can hear distant whispers that seem to come from nowhere and everywhere all at once. The entire setting feels otherworldly, as if you have stepped into a realm where the boundaries between the material and the infernal blur. 
}}












Should a creature willingly let their own blood into the bowl using the sacrificial blade, roll a 1d6 to determine the outcome (the items do not function outside of the watchblock):

1. The ability to speak Infernal is bestowed upon them until their departure from Dis.::

2. They are endowed with Darkvision up to 60 feet for the duration of their stay in Dis.::

3. They acquire a flying speed of 50 feet until they exit Dis.::

4. They obtain resistance to fire damage for as long as they remain in Dis.::

5. Dispater himself grants them Inspiration.::

6. They are afflicted with a madness effect; to determine the specifics, roll on the Long-Term Madness Table, found on page 260 of the DMG. This condition persists until the creature departs from Dis and is impervious to other forms of cure.::


::

### Ominous Portal
Read or paraphrase the following:


{{descriptive
A towering portal looms before you, its monolithic frame stretching at least 250 feet into the infernal sky. Carved from ancient stone, its surface is etched with cryptic symbols that seem to writhe when viewed too long, as if alive.

At its base, rivers of molten lava churn, casting a sulfurous glow that makes the carvings pulse with malevolent energy. The very air thrums with ancient power, whispers of forgotten rituals slithering through the heat, beckoning you forward . . . or warning you away.

Standing before this monolithic structure, you feel the palpable heat and the weight of countless centuries that this portal has overseen.
}}






This portal serves as a teleportation gateway, linked to select sister portals across Dis. One leads to the Iron Tower, where Dispater holds court, while another connects to the Great Prison of Mentiri.

Activating the portal is no mere feat; it requires a complex litany of command words to be spoken in the guttural tones of Infernal.
As the final word is spoken, the stone gateway shudders, releasing a deep, grinding groan. A thin fissure of infernal light splits down its center, widening as the massive slabs slide apart.

A sinister force ripples outward, its insidious pull tugging at the souls of those within 30 feet. It urges but does not command, a whisper of temptation lingering at the edges of the mind—easily ignored by the strong-willed . . . if they choose.

For those daring enough to step through, focus is paramount. The traveler must concentrate on the desired sister portal to ensure safe passage. A lapse invites catastrophe—a torrent of psychic agony ravages the mind, dealing 12d8 psychic damage. Those who falter are violently expelled, crashing to the ground prone.

**Developments.** Consider placing one of these portals on the heroes' path. It could be a strategic move by Asmodeus himself, the tempting fruit of expedient travel, or something more sinister.

Consider a scene where the party stumbles upon a greater devil or another high-ranking NPC in the midst of activating the portal. Concealed, or hidden in plain sight, the heroes might be close enough to discern the arcane utterances required to open the gateway.

### Blackrock Hole
:

Dispater and his cohorts have amassed a diverse workforce of humanoids skilled in the arts of excavation and mining. This labor force includes dwarves, duergar and azer. Those who defy orders, attempt escape, or rise in rebellion are confined within this dreadful place, subjected to relentless torment until they are either broken into submission or meet their demise. Once they are deemed sufficiently compliant they are sent back to the mines to resume their toil.

Should your heroes be on a quest to liberate a specific NPC or reclaim a lost soul, it is possible that they may be here.

The only access to the holding facility is a vertical shaft that bores into the hellish mountain, plummeting 150 feet into darkness. 

Read or paraphrase the following:

:
{{descriptive
The shaft is a gaping maw cut into the heart of the hellish mountain, a roughly circular aperture that spans 50 feet across. Its edges are jagged, lined with sharp rocks that jut out like the teeth of some gargantuan beast. Toxic plumes of gas belch from fissures around the shaft, painting the air with a palette of noxious fumes that twist and writhe before disappearing into the oppressive atmosphere.

This daunting orifice dives aggressively into the bowels of the mountain, a descent that swallows light and hope alike. The deeper it plunges, the more it ensnares itself in an impenetrable darkness that seems almost alive, a darkness that breathes and beckons to those peering into its depths. 

The shaft promises a journey into the unknown, a path that may lead to untold secrets or unspeakable doom, lying in wait in the cavernous underbelly of this infernal landscape.
}}

::

In the grim expanse of Blackrock Hole, **claw devils** serve as the sinister porters, tasked with the transport of captives within the facility. These devils are known for their malevolence, often engaging in the malicious act of 'accidentally' releasing their grip on those they are supposed to carry. This cruel habit is the reason why Area 1 is carpeted with the bones of the unfortunate souls who have plummeted to their demise. 
::

### Numbered Areas
::
##### 1. Access Shaft
Read or paraphrase the following:



{{descriptive
You see the 50-foot-wide shaft conclude at a floor of black basalt, strewn with the bones of humanoids from every age. Many of the bones are bleached white with age, while others are gruesomely fresh, clinging still to remnants of flesh and sinew. All bear the brutal marks of trauma, broken and shattered in countless places. The stench of decay hangs heavily in the air, nearly overpowering you with its intensity. 
}}


A successful DC 20 Investigation check reveals an *Ioun Stone of Insight* within a shattered dwarvish skull.

There is a 50% chance that a pair of **claw devil** are here picking over the bones. They are bored and eager to fight. 

::




##### 2. Ring of Alcoves


The ring is notched with numerous 10 foot square alcoves, which are used mainly for storage. These alcoves may contain:

1. **Provisions.** Crates holding foodstuffs for humanoids, dried rations and waterskins of barely drinkable water. After all, the prisoners need to eat. 
:
2. **Torture Instruments.** The alcove houses a collection of infernal devices designed to extract information or enforce obedience by inflicting pain. 
:
3. **Office.** This particular alcove boasts an array of comfortable furnishings, among them a silk folding cot and a writing desk crafted from sleek black marble. The complex falls under the watchful eye of a human **mage** named Darius Shelton. Darius' responsibilities include meticulous record-keeping of inmate transgressions and their sentences. His duties are supported by Grul, a human bodyguard with a **gladiator's** prowess, distinguished by his silence due to a lack of a tongue. Grul keeps a *Potion of Speed* on his person at all times and will quaff it if threatened. Additionally, Darius' operations are facilitated by a trio of **imps**, who serve diligently as messengers.
Darius has 1d4+1 Common magical items kept in his desk, rolled randomly (only choose items that will fit in a drawer). 
Also, 1d4 vials of *Truth Serum* (DMG, page 258) will be found here. 
The drawer is not locked but trapped with a poison needle. It is	coated with purple wyrm poison (DMG, page 258), DC 20 Investigation check to discover. 
:
4. **Grul's Quarters.** The alcove contains mundane furniture and a carpet woven of worg hide. Grul keeps two more *Potions of Speed* in a secret compartment beneath the rug. A successful DC 18 Perception or Investigation check to discover these items.

:
**Confiscated Gear.** Within this alcove, a multitude of crates and chests are piled high around the room's perimeter. While many are empty, several hold an assortment of mundane equipment taken from prisoners. This collection includes non-magical armor, weapons and various pieces of adventuring gear as detailed in the PHB. Should the heroes find themselves detained here, their belongings would be stored among these items. Darius makes it a routine to sift through the amassed gear, selecting the most valuable pieces for himself. 
:
**Developments.**
If the pair of **claw devils** were not encountered in the access shaft, Area 1, they will be patrolling the circle.


::

##### 3. Red Wing
The entrance to this chamber is secured by a massive door, a 2-foot-thick slab of black basalt, measuring 7 feet in width and towering 12 feet high. Emblazoned upon this formidable portal are fiendish runes emitting a reddish glow.
The runes inscribed on the door are characters from the Infernal alphabet. To unlock the door, one must touch the runes that spell out *"Dispater"* in the correct order. This sequence is a well-known secret among all NPCs and devils within the facility.





Should an incorrect sequence be input, malice gas, as described in DMG, page 258, is emitted from a central circular depression in the door. This gas affects any creature within a 20-foot radius, requiring a DC 20 Investigation check to discern the trap's mechanism. In addition to releasing the gas, a piercing alarm that resembles the screech of a claw devil is triggered, echoing throughout the facility and putting all its inhabitants on high alert.
The door has 150 hit points, an AC of 18, and a damage threshold of 20. There is enough gas stored within the door for 3 uses. 










Within the wing are 10 cells, each barred with a sturdy wooden door reinforced with iron bands. An infernal **iron golem** guards the wing, programmed to attack intruders. The golem has the same stats as a iron golem (MM, page 170) but has a reddish cast to its body.

High profile, dangerous captives are housed here. An iron key hangs on wall within the wing, its boss cast in the form of a leering skull. 

\page


{{pageNumber,auto}}






Examples of NPCs/creatures held in these cells include:

1. 7 **azer** led by one named Prince Brondar Forgeheart. Dispater and his cronies covet their unmatched metalworking skills. Despite enduring brutal torture and the bleak confines of their cells, Brondar and his people refuse to succumb to Dispater's demands to work in his mines and divulge their ancient crafting secrets. Their spirits, though battered, remain unbroken; their refusal to bend is a testament to their indomitable will and loyalty to their elemental essence. If released, Brondar offers to serve the heroes until their quest is complete, although he orders his kin to attempt to return to their native elemental plane. 
:
2. Prince Thrain Understone, once a paragon of **duergar** resilience, has been warped by ceaseless torment into a specter of madness. When heroes find him, they encounter a prince who has lost everything but the echo of his name. 
As the heroes draw near, Prince Thrain mutters disjointedly, his voice a chilling blend of despair and madness:

:: *"The forge . . . the forge burns cold now, yet my mind blazes with the fires of a thousand hells. Do you see the flames too?"*

:: *"Understone, they called me, unbreakable, unyielding. But what is unbreakable to the endless tide? What is a name in the abyss?"*

:: *"They sought the keys to the kingdom, the secrets buried beneath. But what are secrets to the lost? What kingdoms do we rule?"*

:: *"Once a prince but now a plaything of shadows. The crown weighs heavy on a head filled with whispers. Will you bear it with me, or shall we cast it into the fire together?"*

##### 4. Black Wing

The entrance to this wing is marked by a normal, albeit oversized, stone door. 

A solitary **flesh golem** silently patrols this area. This golem features the head of a dwarf and an assortment of limbs and body parts from other creatures that reside here. The slaves call it Patchwork (pictured), and are terrified of its cruelty.

This section of the prison houses ordinary prisoners in squalid and overcrowded conditions. The stench in this wing is unbearable, a foul mix of body odor and excrement. 

Eight out of the ten cells contain 3d6 creatures; roll a d6 to determine:

1. Dwarves from Mithral Hall
2. Dwarves from Sundabar
3. Duergar from the Underdark
4. Rock gnomes 
5. Deep gnomes (Svirfneblin)
6. Kobolds.  The heroes might be surprised to find these creatures here, yet kobolds have a strong inclination towards mining. They search for precious metals and gems, often serving dragons and hoping to find treasures to appease their draconic patrons. 
	
If the heroes succeed in defeating Patchwork, any Charisma-based ability checks they make to negotiate with the prisoners will be conducted with advantage. 

All of these prisoners have suffered greatly, their health deteriorated from maltreatment. 

Darius Shelton aims to rehabilitate these prisoners and deploy them back to the nearby mines as slaves, although he is keenly aware that many are in such dire condition that they may not survive much longer. 

Should the heroes choose to liberate these slaves, they face a dilemma regarding their future care, a decision that falls to the Dungeon Master's discretion. There is a chance that Prince Brondar and his Azer kin could intervene, offering guidance and a potential solution to this predicament. 

The remaining two cells are meant to store basic provisions for the slaves but these supplies are mostly rotting.

##### 4. Monster Wing



The entrance to this wing features a 3-foot-thick, one-way glass slab measuring 7 feet wide by 12 feet tall, allowing visibility from only one side. Tiny holes puncture the door to permit air flow. The door operates with the command word *"Grul"*. Creatures capable of transforming into  gaseous form can pass through these small openings. 

Darius Shelton reserves this wing for an assortment of monsters that capture his interest, which he intends to study during his leisure. They have all been subjected to experiments, resulting in some becoming deranged versions of their former selves. They exhibit unpredictable behavior and are prone to bouts of violence and paranoia. 

The wing is patrolled by a **wereboar** known as Eldrick Greymantle, who is often found in his human guise. His allegiance lies with Darius and he is prepared to alert the facility's other inhabitants at the first sign of trouble. Eldrick may also play the role of a tour guide to buy himself time, offering visitors an escorted look at the creatures detained here, much like a curator in a zoo. Eldrick holds the keys to each of the cells. 
:
A few lines of dialogue from Eldrick might include:

:: *"Ah,  welcome! I'm Eldrick Greymantle, your guide to this most unique collection. Please, follow me and keep your hands to yourselves. Some of our residents are . . . less than hospitable."*

:: *"This way, if you please. Each creature you see has been carefully studied by Master Darius himself. Fascinating, aren't they?"*

:: *"Oh, you mustn't worry about me. I've been here long enough to know how to handle our more temperamental guests. Besides, I have my own means of protection, should the need arise."*
:
Creatures held here include:

1. A **gibbering mouther.** Darius is fascinated by this creature and hopes to graft its form onto a flesh golem.
:
2. A **medusa.** Known as Venestra, this medusa has been rendered blind, her eyes seared from their sockets, her petrifying gaze ineffective. Only powerful magic, such as a *Greater Restoration* spell, can return her sight. Venestra is open to the idea of forming an alliance with the heroes if it means she can take down Darius and lay waste to the facility, provided the heroes are brave enough to join forces with her. 
:
3. A **mimic.** The mimic is currently in the form of a stone block, hoping to avoid the attention of its captors. Darius recently acquired the mimic and is currently studying its behavior.
:
4. A **doppelganger.** When encountered, the creature will have taken the form of Grul. It does its best to bluff its way into the heroes' confidence.
:
5. **Optional.** A NPC of importance to the heroes.

::

### Molten Fall
:
The Molten Fall is a terrifying spectacle, a cascade of glowing lava that plummets 1000 feet down the sheer face of the mountain, embodying the raw power of nature's fury.

The lava flows like a river of fire, glowing in shades of orange, red and yellow. As it tumbles over the cliff's edge, the lava divides into numerous streams. The air around the fall is thick, warping the light and casting eerie shadows on the surrounding landscape.

The sound of the Molten Fall is equally captivating—a deep, resonant rumble accompanied by the hiss and crackle of cooling lava as it meets the air. Steam billows from where the molten rock contacts the cooler ground at the base of the fall, shrouding the area in a mist that glows with the reflection of the fiery cascade.
::

### Skulltop
:
Perched near the summit lies an awe-inspiring sight: an immense skull, sculpted from the mountain's own molten black basalt. This monolithic relic of a skull appears almost to leer at those who dare approach. Perhaps a guardian of ancient mysteries or a monument to a forgotten god, its formation remains a secret of the unknown. 

The mountain itself, a colossal structure, is cloaked in the darkness of the basalt from which it rises, adding an air of foreboding to the scene. A winding path, as treacherous as it is narrow, snakes up the mountain's flank. It is a route that tests the mettle of any who traverse it, flanked by sheer drops and perilous turns. This path, seemingly carved by forces both ancient and unfathomable, invites the brave, the reckless and the foolhardy alike.

### Area Locations

##### 1. Ichor Pools
Read or paraphrase the following:


{{descriptive
You see pools of dark ichor scattered across the ground. The ichor resembles thick tar, shimmering with an uncanny sheen under the dim light. Each pool moves subtly, almost as if it breathes, its surface rippling without any breeze to disturb it.

As you look closer, you notice the eerie way these pools reflect the scant light, creating a halo of grim luminescence around them. The air feels heavier as you approach, charged with a malevolent energy that seems to seep from the very ground. A faint, indescribable odor emanates from the pools, adding to the unsettling atmosphere.

}}



The ceiling arches into a dome, reaching up to nearly 100 feet at its peak. Perched on uneven ledges high above, two **claw devils** lie in wait, ready to pounce on any who trespass. Leveraging their ability to grapple, they swoop down, seize their prey and then ascend only to release their hold, letting their victims plummet into pits below. When a creature is dropped into a pool by these devils, the damage from the fall is lessened, dealing only a 1d4 of damage for every 10 feet fallen.

The pools are 2 to 5 feet deep and brim with demon ichor. Any creature that touches the ichor is required to succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or endure an effect, determined by a random 1d6 roll on the table below:
:
1. **Eyes Multiply.** The creature's face becomes covered in small, blinking eyes, granting them a terrifying appearance. While these eyes might offer enhanced visual perception, they also cause intense migraines and difficulty focusing on specific tasks.

*Mechanical effects:* Advantage on Wisdom

(Perception) checks that rely on sight. Disadvantage

on any task requiring concentration, including

spellcasting, due to migraines and overwhelming 

input.
::
2. **Glowing Fungus Growth.** Luminescent fungi start growing on the creature's skin, emitting a soft, eerie light. This makes stealth nearly impossible in dark environments but might provide a minor light source.

*Mechanical effects:* The creature emits dim light in a 

5-foot radius, negating the ability to hide in darkness.
:

3. **Ink Blood.** The creature's blood turns into  thick, black ink, leaving dark stains on their skin and making their wounds alarmingly visible. This change has no mechanical effect.
::
4. **Melodic Voice.** The creature's voice becomes unnaturally melodic, to the point where ordinary speech is replaced by song. This can enchant listeners but makes it impossible to lie as the truth resonates within their melody.

*Mechanical effects:* Advantage on Performance 

checks when using their voice. Disadvantage on 

Deception checks, as their true intentions become 

clear through their song.
::
5. **Crystallized Teeth.** The creature's teeth turn into sharp, translucent crystals.

*Mechanical effects:*  The creature can use their action

to make a bite attack, a successful hit doing 1d6 

piercing damage plus the creature's Strength 

modifier. Additionally, the creature can crush rocks

and even gemstones in their mouth.
::
6. **Toadstool Appendage.** One of the creature's arms ends in a large, toadstool-like growth instead of a hand, making the appendage non-functional for grasping or wielding items. The toadstool emits spores periodically, which have no effect but add to the creature's peculiar appearance.

*Mechanical effects:* The creature cannot use two-

handed weapons or items that require two hands to 

operate. Disadvantage on ability checks, attack rolls, 

and saving throws that involve the use of hands, such 

as Climbing, Sleight of Hand, or using tools with that 

arm.

:
These alterations remain in effect until reversed by powerful magic such as *Greater Restoration*, *Wish* or equivalent spells.

##### 2. Altar of Flesh
Read or paraphrase the following:

{{descriptive
Within the confines of this sombre chamber, the floor lies shrouded under an inch of sticky blood, a macabre carpet that clings to the soles of any who dare tread here. This blood oozes from an altar at the room's heart, an unsettling construct of flesh. This altar is marred by scars, each a silent witness to the countless ceremonies it has endured. 

The air is heavy, charged with an ominous energy that seems to emanate from the altar. The light flickers across the crimson floor, casting eerie shadows that play along the walls, further deepening the sense of unease that permeates this unhallowed place.
}}





In this dark chamber, a sinister trap is laid. If a creature approaches within 10 feet of the altar, the altar speaks telepathically to them with the voices of loved ones, friends and perhaps those long lost to death, creating an illusion of familiarity and trust. These voices implore the heroes to cut them free from their confinement within the altar's grotesque form.

To resist this compelling call, a creature must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or feel an overwhelming obligation to liberate their supposed loved ones by cutting into the altar. This save can be repeated at the end of the creature's turn to end the effect (immune for 24 hours).

However, this act of compassion triggers a deadly consequence: touching the altar causes the ceiling in the 50-foot passage to the south to collapse, sealing it off completely. Those within the passage sense a foreboding rumble overhead. They must quickly declare their intent to flee, or suffer crushing defeat under 20d10 bludgeoning damage, finding themselves trapped beneath rubble, unable to breathe or move and sustaining an additional 10d10 damage each turn. Fleeing creatures have the choice to move forward or retreat. 

If the altar sustains any piercing or slashing damage, it bleeds profusely, adding to the blood on the floor, which rises by 1 foot each turn, potentially reaching up to 20 feet towards the ceiling. Each new wound inflicted on the altar accelerates the blood's ascent, increasing the rate by an additional foot per turn for every wound. Thus, four wounds would cause the blood level to rise by 4 feet each turn, threatening to drown the chamber in a tide of gore. The altar has an AC of 10 for the purposes of being hit.

Every 15 points of healing done to the altar seals one wound and reduces the flow of blood by 1 foot/turn. 
:::::









##### 3. Hall of Statues
Read or paraphrase the following:

{{descriptive
You see a curtain of blood-red silk, guarding the archway to the chamber ahead. This fabric, heavy and ominous, is intricately stitched with a grand depiction of Dispater leering at those who approach, his image woven into the silk with such detail that it seems almost alive in the dim light.

Beyond this foreboding tapestry lies a hall, where two lines of statues carved from  basalt stand in silent vigil. These statues are humanoid in shape, yet strikingly featureless, as if the sculptor left their work unfinished. The absence of features adds to the eerie atmosphere, their smooth surfaces absorbing the light, casting deep shadows across the hall. 

At the far end of the room, you see a stack of metal ingots, each one forged from a reddish iron that seems to smolder with an inner fire. The metal is streaked with what appear to be veins, giving the ingots an almost living appearance. The stack is neatly arranged and invokes a sense of unease, as though the very essence of the infernal planes has been captured and solidified into these bars of metal.
}}






The infernal ingots are an illusion; a successful DC 18 Intelligence check reveals their true nature. Any creature that touches the ingots or one of the statues activates the room's trap.

This trap transforms the featureless statues into **flesh golems**, one for each hero present in the room. These golems are not mere replicas; they subtly vary in appearance, each adopting the mannerisms and subtle features of a different hero, creating an eerie mirror of the party. 

The twist . . . a golem can only be damaged or affected by the hero it mimics.
To discern which golem corresponds to which hero, a successful DC 16 Perception check is required, which can be attempted as a bonus action. This realization is crucial for the heroes to effectively combat their golem counterparts. Without this knowledge, the heroes must resort to trial and error, observing the effects of their attacks to determine which golems they can harm. 





\page



{{pageNumber,auto}}




### Infernal Ingots



{{imageMaskEdge3,--offset:-36%,--rotation:-12
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/JnuEtpu.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->


\page





### Altar of Flesh




{{pageNumber,auto}}



{{imageMaskEdge3,--offset:-36%,--rotation:-12
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/lQGRSVI.png){height:100%}
}}

     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->




\page




### Hall of Statues




{{pageNumber,auto}}



{{imageMaskEdge3,--offset:-36%,--rotation:-12
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/YIfn5z2.png){height:100%}
}}

     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->









\page



{{pageNumber,auto}}



##### 4. Chamber of Opposition
Read or paraphrase the following:

{{descriptive
You see before you a vast room, its central floor dominated by a huge hexagonal silver mirror, measuring nearly 30 by 40 feet. The air here is unnaturally cold, sending shivers down your spine as you step forward. Whispers, too soft to decipher, fill the space, suggesting the presence of some unseen spirit. The mirror itself lies flat against the ground, its surface so polished that it seems almost a portal to another realm, rather than a mere object. The walls around you are bare, focusing all attention on the mirror. The ethereal whispers seem to emanate from the mirror, as if it were communicating with the spirits of another world. You feel the atmosphere heavy with anticipation, waiting for something . . .
}}




Positioned flat on the chamber's floor, the mirror operates as a *Mirror of Opposition*. If any creature views its reflection within it—requiring them to be near or directly over the mirror—a stark flash of white light erupts, impacting everyone present, extending to those on the northern landing. 

This spectacle gives rise to **doppelgängers**, not as identical replicas but as grotesque, ghoulish versions of the observers. These sinister doubles, their forms distorted and intentions malevolent, appear to emerge directly from the mirror, poised to attack their living counterparts with a viciousness derived from the depths of the netherworld. If a duplicate kills its original, it disappears immediately after.

The mirror only functions once per day.















::
##### 5. Sas' Throne Room
Read or paraphrase the following:


{{descriptive
Before you is a grand throne room, its vastness accentuated by the high ceiling, 30 feet above, where mephits—creatures of lava and steam—swirl in a chaotic dance. The air is thick with the heat of the underworld, the scent of sulfur hanging heavily. At the far end, upon a throne carved from obsidian, sits Sas, a female erinyes devil. Her wings, dark and imposing, are spread wide, and her gaze, as she spots you, is both calculating and amused.
By her feet, a hell hound with a mane of fire lies growling, its eyes fixed upon you with malevolent intent. Sas, unfazed, strokes its mane casually, a sinister smile playing on her lips.
}}

:::

{{descriptive
"Well, well, what do we have here?" she purrs, her voice smooth yet laced with danger. "Heroes, come to challenge the depths of Hell itself? Or are you merely lost lambs, ready for the slaughter?"
She leans forward, her interest piqued, her smile widening. "I've been waiting for some . . . entertainment. And here you are, delivering yourselves to my doorstep. Let's play a little game, shall we? I do so love to toy with my prey. Tell me, heroes, what brought you to my realm? Hope? Desperation? Or is it sheer folly?"
The hell hound by her feet lets out a low, threatening growl, as if echoing her sentiment, while the mephits above seem to circle closer, as though drawn by the tension in the air.
}}




The following foes are present in the chamber:

:: - Sas, a female **erinyes devil** 
:: - 1 **hell hound** named Sebastian
:: - 3 **lava mephits**
:: - 3 **steam mephits** 


:

Sebastian wears a collar of platinum studded with jet stones worth 5,000 gp. 

Sas' throne has a secret compartment that can be found with a successful DC 20 Investigation check. It contains 4d6 Soul Coins and a desiccated humanoid finger in a sealed jar (see below).

::

#### Digit of Percival the Odd
*Wondrous item, rare (requires attunement)*
:
This finger possesses magical properties and once belonged to Percival the Odd, a master thief renowned for his craft. Should a humanoid choose to replace one of their own fingers with this relic, by severing it and affixing the finger to the stump, the flesh miraculously binds, integrating the finger as if it were their own. This grants the bearer a +2 bonus on all ability checks requiring precise manual dexterity, such as Sleight of Hand and Lockpicking.

However, this boon comes with a haunting drawback. The recipient of the finger inherits fragmented memories of Percival's grim fate, executed by hanging in a public square. Consequently, whenever the bearer attempts to take a long rest, they must roll a 1d20; on a roll of 1, they are plagued by vivid nightmares of this execution, preventing any benefit from the rest.









\page





{{pageNumber,auto}}






### Sas


{{imageMaskEdge3,--offset:-36%,--rotation:-12
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/hOeUWuT.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->












\page


{{pageNumber,auto}}

##### Other Rooms
Not every room is detailed in the complex but here are some examples of rooms to add:

1. **False Treasure Chamber.** Twelve chests stacked neatly, half of them are **mimics** that attack as a pack.
:
2. **Treasure Chamber.** Twelve robust metal chests are filled with 20 infernal ingots each, with every ingot weighing 10 pounds. Should the heroes manage to transport them, these ingots could serve as valuable bargaining chips within the Nine Hells. Outside of this infernal realm, however, the ingots hold little value.
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
3. **Wandering Magma Cubes.** These oozes act as dungeon cleaners akin to gelatinous cubes (see below for statistics). 
:
4. **An Unexpected Ally.** A **young silver dragon** named Lunarise is imprisoned within the complex. It is probable that she will be met in the guise of a female elf, a form she prefers to assume while in captivity. Sas, treating her more as a possession than a being, ensures her compliance by keeping her two offspring, silver dragon wyrmlings named Argenthrix and Silvyrith, captive in a separate section of the mountain. Lunarise's alliance with the heroes hinges on the guarantee of her children's safety, without which she remains reluctant to aid them. 




{{imageMaskEdge3,--offset:-12%,--rotation:0
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/oVoxeQV.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->


















\page


{{pageNumber,auto}}


{{monster,frame
## Magma Cube (from the book: *Fiery Denizens*)
*Large ooze, unaligned*
___
**Armor Class** :: 8
**Hit Points**  :: 114 (12d10 + 48)
**Speed**       :: 15 ft.
___
|  STR  |  DEX  |  CON  |  INT  |  WIS  |  CHA  |
|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|
|17 (+3)|6 (-2)|18 (+4)|1 (-5)|6 (-2)|1 (-5)|
___


**Damage Immunities** :: fire
**Condition Immunities** :: blinded, charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, prone
**Senses**               :: blindsight 60 ft. (blind beyond this radius), passive Perception 8
**Languages**            :: -
**Challenge**            :: 4 (1,100 XP)
___
***Amorphous.*** The magma cube can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.
:
***Fire Absorption.*** Whenever the magma cube is subjected to fire damage, it takes no damage and instead regains a number of hit points equal to the fire damage dealt.

:
***Heated Body.*** A creature that touches the magma cube or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 7 (2d6) fire damage.

:
***Equipment Destruction.***  Any non-magical melee weapon that hits the magma cube is destroyed unless the weapon's user succeeds on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. Non-magical armor or equipment worn by a creature engulfed by the magma cube is destroyed at the end of the creature's turn.




### Actions
***Engulf.*** As an action, the magma cube moves up to its speed. While doing so, it can enter Large or smaller creatures' spaces. Whenever the magma cube enters a creature's space, the creature must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is engulfed; the engulfed creature is blinded, restrained, and unable to breathe, and it must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw at the start of each of the magma cube's turns or take 21 (6d6) fire damage. If the magma cube moves, the engulfed creature moves with it. An engulfed creature can try to escape by taking an action to make a DC 15 Strength check. On a success, the creature escapes and enters a space of its choice within 5 feet of the cube.
:
***Pseudopod.*** *Melee Weapon Attack:* +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. *Hit:* 13 (2d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage. 








}}

::::::::::



##### Ecology

The magma cube, unlike its gelatinous cousins, lacks the qualities of stealth and transparency. However, it more than compensates for these shortcomings with its unique and destructive abilities. These oozes are the formidable 'cleaners' of subterranean caverns and passages beneath volcanic regions. They have evolved distinct traits to counterbalance their lack of speed and stealth.

One of their most favored ambush tactics involves positioning themselves in areas where unsuspecting prey is likely to drop or fall onto them. In such scenarios, they prove to be exceptionally deadly. Their ability to destroy non-magical equipment and inflict severe damage makes them a significant threat. 

The magma cube's strategy capitalizes on the element of surprise and their environmental advantage, turning their apparent weaknesses into lethal strengths in their natural, fiery habitats.





\page

{{backCover}}

# Dis
:
Welcome to Dis, where the iron must flow.
:
Beneath the choking skies of the second layer of Hell, the colossal Ironthrone Mountain looms—a volcanic crucible where molten rivers carve through jagged obsidian cliffs, and the very air hums with the screams of the damned. Here, infernal iron is mined and forged into war machines, soul coins and weapons of ruin, all fueling the ceaseless machinations of Hell.
:
This mini-campaign plunges your heroes into the depths of Ironthrone Mountain, where treacherous paths, imposing devilish overseers and ruthless ambitions make survival uncertain. 
:
Will they escape enslavement, rescue the lost, or strike a deadly blow against Sas, the merciless erinyes who rules this infernal foundry?
:
Forges burn, chains tighten and the iron must flow—will you fight against the tide, or be consumed by it?
___

arguments.content:
{{frontCover}}


# Dis: Montaña Trono de Hierro
## Ampliando Chains of Asmodeus (nivel 12)
___

{{banner Christopher Hamwell}}

{{footnote
 *El hierro debe fluir . . .* 
}}

![background image](https://i.imgur.com/VL02ICR.png){position:absolute,bottom:0,left:0,height:100%}

\page



{{pageNumber,auto}}




## Dis: Montaña Trono de Hierro

 
{{quote

*El hierro debe fluir . . .* 


}}



Lee lo siguiente y parafrásalo a tus jugadores como trasfondo:




{{descriptive
Ante vosotros se despliega un paisaje imponente. Las montañas, compuestas enteramente de hierro dentado, se alzan hacia cielos cargados de humo, sus siluetas como testimonio de la artesanía infernal que domina este reino. Este no es un mundo natural, sino una fragua de condenación, donde la propia tierra ha sido retorcida para servir a los oscuros caprichos de su gobernante, Dispater.

El aire es denso, no solo por el calor opresivo sino también por el olor acre del metal ardiendo. Cuanto más avanzáis, más afirma su malevolencia el paisaje, con espejismos que bailan en el horizonte y difuminan la línea entre realidad e ilusión.

Al acercaros a una de las muchas minas de hierro, los sonidos de la industria y del sufrimiento alcanzan vuestros oídos, creciendo a cada paso. La cacofonía es incesante: el clangor metálico de los picos golpeando el hierro, el gemido de la tierra obligada a entregar su botín infernal y el rugido lejano de los hornos devorando mineral. Esas son las sinfonías de Dis, composiciones de fatiga y tormento, interpretadas por las almas condenadas a trabajar eternamente en estas fosas.

La entrada de la mina se presenta como una herida abierta en el costado de la montaña. Al aventuraros dentro, os recibe la visión de túneles estrechos iluminados por la luz vacilante de antorchas de fuego de alma. Estas antorchas proyectan sombras sobre los muros excavados con rudeza, creando un laberinto cambiante de oscuridad y luz.

El aire dentro de la mina es pesado, no solo por el agotamiento físico, sino por el peso de la esperanza perdida, mientras las almas condenadas a este destino continúan su labor, con el tintineo de sus cadenas marcando un ritmo sombrío que punctúa el silencio de la rendición. Aquí el tiempo pierde significado. El ciclo implacable de trabajo y breves períodos de descanso, bajo la mirada vigilante de capataces demoníacos, se difumina en una eternidad monótona. 


A pesar de la sensación omnipresente de desesperanza, hay una belleza extraña en el paisaje infernal que os rodea. El resplandor del hierro fundido corriendo en regueros por los suelos de los túneles proyecta una luz sobrenatural, mientras las chispas de las fraguas se alzan hacia arriba, iluminando por un instante la oscuridad antes de extinguirse en la nada.

}}



::::::

{{descriptive
Esto es Dis, donde cada golpe de martillo y cada carro de mineral extraído son testimonio de la voluntad indomable de su soberano infernal. Es un lugar de paradojas: de fuerza hallada en momentos de debilidad, de camaradería forjada en las profundidades del aislamiento y de belleza vislumbrada en el corazón de la oscuridad.
}}




::

### Trasfondo
:
Esta mini-campaña se desarrolla en y alrededor del ominoso telón de fondo de la Montaña Trono de Hierro, un volcán colosal con ríos de lava cayendo por sus laderas (consulta el mapa más abajo para ver las localizaciones de aventura). 

Senderos traicioneros serpentean hacia arriba y hacia abajo por la montaña, ofreciendo rutas difíciles para aquellos que carecen de alas o de transporte mágico. Esta montaña es solo una de varias donde se extrae hierro infernal y se transforma en lingotes, destinados a la construcción de máquinas de guerra, soul coins y la creación de armamento blandido por toda clase de entidades diabólicas. 
:
Estas aventuras están diseñadas para cuatro personajes de nivel 12. 
::




### Ganchos de aventura
:
Puede haber varias razones por las que tus héroes viajen a la Montaña Trono de Hierro. Considera las siguientes opciones:

1. **Encarcelamiento.** Si tus héroes acaban capturados en otro rincón de los Nueve Infiernos, en lugar de enfrentarse a una muerte segura, podrían ser perdonados y enviados a las minas como esclavos. Considera Blackrock Hole como destino de tus jugadores.
:
2. **Misión de Rescate.** Tus héroes podrían emprender un peligroso viaje a la montaña con el objetivo de rescatar a un PNJ retenido contra su voluntad, o de recuperar una filacteria. En ese caso, su presa probablemente se encontrará en Skulltop.
:
3. **Venganza contra Sas.** La Montaña Trono de Hierro está gobernada por una despiadada erinyes llamada Sas. Tus héroes podrían guardarle un profundo rencor, o tal vez recibir un lucrativo contrato para eliminarla. De nuevo, Skulltop es el lugar al que deben ir.
:
4. **Las Minas.** Esta región está salpicada de numerosas minas de hierro, donde esclavos trabajan y mueren sin que sus vidas importen demasiado. Aunque esas minas no se desarrollan en este documento, ofrecen una buena oportunidad para ampliar el contenido.

El conjunto de localizaciones de aventura y encuentros descritos aquí es flexible y puede incorporarse a tu campaña según desees, permitiéndote enriquecer y ampliar tu propia narrativa integrándolos donde mejor encajen. 
:
Sea cual sea la motivación tras el viaje a la montaña, tus jugadores deben comprender una regla fundamental: *El hierro debe fluir . . .* 



::
## Localizaciones de aventura


:

### El Anillo de Fuego
:
Si los héroes deciden acercarse a la montaña, deberán atravesar la extensión relativamente abierta conocida como el Anillo de Fuego. 

Esta zona, aunque transitable sin protección mágica contra el fuego, es extremadamente calurosa y opresiva. Está salpicada de patrullas de imps y spined devils atentos a esclavos fugados, lemures escaqueándose de sus deberes o cualquier amenaza a la seguridad de la montaña. 



{{descriptive
A medida que os aventuráis más cerca del corazón del paisaje infernal, la silueta ominosa de la montaña se cierne sobre vosotros, proyectando su sombra sobre la peligrosa extensión conocida como el Anillo de Fuego. El aire se vuelve más caliente, espeso por el olor a azufre y ceniza, haciendo de cada aliento una tarea laboriosa. El suelo bajo vuestros pies está marcado por el flujo implacable de corrientes de lava.

El terreno es traicionero, sembrado de socavones que aparecen sin previo aviso, cuyas fauces abiertas engullen a cualquier cosa o persona lo bastante desafortunada como para acercarse demasiado. Chorros de lava brotan esporádicamente de la tierra, lanzando magma al cielo solo para hacerlo caer como granizo ígneo. El calor es opresivo y agota vuestras fuerzas con cada paso.

En medio de este cuadro infernal, los diablos, guardianes de este lugar maldito, patrullan los cielos con ojos atentos e intención malévola. Estos centinelas alados están siempre vigilantes, buscando esclavos fugados, lemures que intentan evitar sus deberes infernales o cualquier amenaza percibida a su dominio. La presencia de estas patrullas demoníacas añade un peligro adicional al ya formidable desafío de atravesar el Anillo de Fuego. 

El viaje a través del Anillo de Fuego no es solo una prueba física, sino también una prueba de voluntad y valor.

}}
:



:::::::::




#### Encuentros del Anillo
:
##### 1. Socavón Infernal
Mientras los héroes avanzan con cautela por el Anillo de Fuego, el suelo bajo ellos delata su presencia con un desplazamiento repentino y traicionero. 

Lee o parafrasea lo siguiente:


{{descriptive
Mientras cruzáis el Anillo de Fuego, el suelo bajo vuestros pies os traiciona de repente. Se forma rápidamente un socavón inmenso, de más de 300 pies de diámetro. Veis rocas y arena negra precipitándose hacia la abertura mientras la tierra se derrumba sobre sí misma. El brillo ominoso de la lava, visible muy abajo, proyecta una luz espectral sobre la escena, anunciando las profundidades mortales que yacen debajo. El aire a vuestro alrededor se espesa con ceniza y azufre, haciendo que cada respiración sea pesada y agobiante.

}}





Da a los dos héroes que vayan en cabeza la oportunidad de hacer una prueba de Perception con una dificultad (CD) 18 para detectar el terreno inestable. 

Si los héroes no advierten el terreno traicionero, cada uno deberá superar una prueba de Acrobatics o Athletics CD 16 para escapar de la atracción del terreno colapsando y del tirón ominoso del socavón. El éxito significa una carrera desesperada hacia un lugar seguro, logrando aferrarse al terreno más estable que rodea la sima. Sin embargo, el fracaso presagia un destino terrible; aquellos incapaces de escapar de las garras del socavón son absorbidos hacia sus profundidades mientras este se cierra sobre ellos, arrastrándolos 30 pies en el primer turno y 10 pies adicionales en cada turno posterior.

Quienes queden atrapados en el socavón en colapso sufren 3d8 de daño de fuego y 3d8 de daño contundente por turno, mientras son golpeados por rocas y abrasados por el intenso calor que emana de abajo. La oscuridad los envuelve, dejándolos cegados e incapacitados. Atrapados por el abrazo de la tierra, se descubren incapaces de hablar o respirar, y su situación empeora a cada instante.

Para aquellos con la mala fortuna de verse arrastrados al socavón, la supervivencia depende de un hilo de esperanza: la posibilidad de contar con algún medio mágico de escape. Sin él, sus perspectivas son sombrías, pues el daño implacable y la oscuridad sofocante no prometen más que un final espantoso.

##### 2. Puente de Basalto
Lee o parafrasea lo siguiente cuando tus héroes se acerquen al puente:



{{descriptive
Os encontráis con un puente de antiguo basalto que cruza un río de lava agitada. La estructura parece casi natural en su formación, como si hubiera sido creada por las mismas fuerzas que moldearon la tierra. 

Al fijaros mejor, el resplandor inquietante de la lava de abajo ilumina runas infernales talladas en la piedra. El aire está cargado de calor y el estruendo del río fundido ruge en vuestros oídos, creando un telón de fondo de furia primordial para esta escena de sombría majestuosidad. 
}}



El puente mide 500 pies de largo y cae 300 pies hacia la lava de abajo. Runas infernales están talladas a lo largo de toda su extensión. Para quienes hablen Infernal o posean medios mágicos para descifrar escritura, los héroes deberán examinar meticulosamente todo el puente para comprender por completo este poema sagrado.



{{note
