### Be Flexible
:


Feel free to design your own battle maps for the pit, tailoring them to the chaotic, shifting nature of Minauros. 

The arena is never truly the same—flooded sections rise and fall, toxic rivers shift course and crumbling platforms collapse mid-fight. You can even alter the layout between bouts, keeping the battlefield dynamic and unpredictable, ensuring no two fights are ever identical.


::






### Accommodation
:
This depends on whether the heroes are enslaved or fighting by choice.


1. **Free.** If your heroes are competing of their own free will, they have the option to stay at Bloodwood Spire (see below). However, feel free to introduce alternative accommodations that better suit your campaign.
:
2. **Slaves.** If enslaved, they are housed in a dungeon complex, complete with a small training pit where they can spar and maintain their combat prowess under watchful eyes.




::




### Bloodwood Spire: A Sanctuary of Decay
:
Should the heroes remain in Minauros of their own free will, securing suitable lodging becomes a pressing necessity. The task falls to Tiax or another benefactor but accommodations in this wretched layer of Hell are scarce. After much effort and significant expense, they manage to acquire an unused, crumbling tower near the arena—Bloodwood Spire.

This ruined structure is twisted and deformed, overtaken by a massive, blackened tree that has burst forth from its core, its twisted limbs clawing at the sky. The entire tree seeps blood, a trickle that drips from its bark and pools along the cracked stone floors. The air is thick with the pungent, metallic scent of iron, so overpowering that it turns the stomach and lingers in the throat.

Read or paraphrase the following: 

:

{{descriptive
Bloodwood Spire looms like a wounded titan, barely visible through Minauros’ shifting mists. A massive, twisted tree has erupted from its core, its bleeding roots splitting stone, its gory branches tearing through shattered windows like jagged spears.

}}


{{imageMaskEdge8,--offset:5%,--rotation:270
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/Cee7EIp.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->







\page

{{pageNumber,auto}}



{{descriptive
The walls weep diluted blood, pooling into crimson puddles that never dry. The twitching roots, like exposed veins, squeeze through broken masonry, as if slowly consuming what remains of the tower. Those inside feel its slow, rhythmic pulse—the heartbeat of the bleeding tree.

The air is thick with rot and iron, the scent of damp wood and fresh blood mingling with the toxic swamp mists. Every step squelches, every breath carries the sting of sickness.

Yet, despite its corruption, the tower offers something rare—sanctuary. From its highest chamber, the arena looms in the distance, its roaring crowds barely audible beneath the slow, dripping heartbeat of the tree.

}}






Despite its horrific, crumbling state, the spire’s height provides an unmatched view of the swamp and city beyond. At night, the poisonous mists pulse with eerie bioluminescence, casting a spectral glow and illuminating its blood-slicked interior.

Living within Bloodwood Spire is not for the faint of heart. Its rooms are cramped and unwelcoming, invaded by the twisting roots and gory branches of the tree. Blood coats every surface.

Imps are drawn to the spire, flitting through the halls to bathe in the dripping blood. They are harmless, indifferent to the heroes’ presence, seemingly content to revel in the endless crimson flow.

There are no threats here unless you choose to introduce them—though nothing in Minauros remains quiet forever.

A combat map of the spire is provided on the page below. 


::


### Gaol and Lesser Pit
:


Should the heroes be captured and and forced to fight in the pit, they will be kept in the Gaol and Lesser Pit (see map below). This location is both a gaol and training facility. It contains a smaller arena where teams can train and be housed under watchful guard. 

This area is described in detail as the heroes are likely be housed here for some time and they may attempt to escape its confines.
:
**Note.** Even if you are not using this facility, it is a good map to keep up your sleeve should the heroes be captured on another layer of Hell.



::




##### Area Descriptions of the Gaol and Lesser Pit   
:
**Note.** The entire structure is infused with a powerful enchantment that prohibits magical egress and entry. Spells and abilities that allow for *Teleportation*, *Dimension Door*, and similar forms of magical travel are rendered ineffective if cast within the facility with the intention of passing through solid stone. However, magic that facilitates limited movement within the confines of the facility remains operational. This selective barrier ensures controlled movement, maintaining the integrity of the facility's security while allowing for certain magical conveniences internally. 
::




  
##### Area 1. Cells
:

{{descriptive
The cells within this section are stark, each spanning 20 feet with stone floors smeared in oily grime. Chains hang forlornly from the walls and ceilings.  
}}




The cell bars, made of robust infernal iron, make any attempt to bend them extremely difficult (Strength checks to bend the bars are at disadvantage, DC 20). Each gate is secured with a pair of sturdy locks (requiring a DC 18 Dexterity check to pick each one).

Prisoners are kept solitary in these cells, spaced as widely apart as the architecture allows. There are 7 such cells arrayed along the southern curve of the facility. The guard chamber, located to the northeast of the most eastern cell, is manned by a duo of **chain devils** (Xalvorr & Yzrak), who take perverse pleasure in tormenting the prisoners with animated chains. Their patience for perceived disobedience is minimal. The chain devils each carry an oversized keyring with individual keys to each cell. The prisoners' belongings are kept under the watch of these devils, who enjoy taking items and flaunting their theft before the prisoners.

At any given moment, one chain devil patrols the cells while the other rests in the guard chamber, though their vigilance wanes, leading to a 20% chance daily that their patrols cease for 2 hours, during which time both will reside in the guard chamber.

Upon the heroes' arrival, the devils will thoroughly search for hidden items like lock picks, necessitating prisoners to succeed in a Sleight of Hand check against the devils' Investigation check to keep such items concealed. Discovery results in days of relentless 'chain therapy.'

However, the captives are well-fed with quality provisions, ensuring they remain in peak condition for the battles that lie ahead.

{{pageNumber,auto}}


  
  

##### 2. Entrance Hall



{{descriptive
The northern wall is adorned with a vast tapestry showcasing Mammon indulging in a feast of demons and delicacies alike, seemingly indifferent to the distinction between them. Encircling the insatiable devil, captives are depicted in chains, while lesser devils offer tributes of food and slaves in a display of unwavering devotion.
}}







{{monster,frame
## Tapestry of Mammon's Feast
*Huge construct, chaotic evil*
___
**Armor Class** :: 15 (natural armor)
**Hit Points**  :: 210 (20d12 + 100)
**Speed**       :: 10 ft., fly 50 ft.
___
|  STR  |  DEX  |  CON  |  INT  |  WIS  |  CHA  |
|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|
|19 (+4)|14 (+2)|20 (+5)|3 (-4)|10 (+0)|1 (-5)|
___
**Saving Throws** :: Dex +7, Con +10
**Damage Resistances** :: bludgeoning, piercing and slashing from nonmagical attacks
**Damage Immunities** :: poison, psychic
**Condition Immunities** :: blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned
**Senses**               :: blindsight 60 ft. (blind beyond this radius), passive Perception 10
**Languages**            :: -
**Challenge**            :: Challenge 12 (8,400 XP)
___
***Antimagic Susceptibility.*** The tapestry is incapacitated while in the area of an antimagic field. If targeted by *Dispel Magic*, the tapestry must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against the caster's spell save DC or fall unconscious for 1 minute.

:
***Damage Transfer.*** While it is grappling a creature, the tapestry takes only half the damage dealt to it, and the creature grappled by the tapestry takes the other half (roll randomly if more than one creature is engulfed).
:
***False Appearance.*** While the tapestry remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a normal tapestry.



### Actions
***Smother.*** *Melee Weapon Attack:* +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. *Hit:* The target is grappled (escape DC 17). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, blinded and at risk of suffocating. Additionally, at the start of the target's turns, the target takes 21 (3d8) bludgeoning damage and 21 (3d8) poison damage.
:
***Regeneration.*** The tapestry can use its action to perform a Mending action, regenerating 2d10 hit points, provided it has not taken fire damage in the last minute. 


}}



:::::::


The tapestry (pictured on following page), which adorns the northern wall, is in truth a sentient construct akin to a *Rug of Smothering*. Any non-fiendish entities approaching from the south are compelled to bow before the rug; failure to do so triggers an attack. It is noteworthy that this rug possesses a fly speed of 50, adding a layer of unpredictability and danger to its already formidable nature. 

This imposing tapestry not only serves as a testament to Mammon's gluttony but also as a deadly guardian, ready to smother and poison those who dare to disrespect or threaten its presence.



::





##### 3. Throne



{{descriptive
The viewing platform, elevated 40 feet above the arena's floor, exudes a greenish, vile aura. Centered upon this platform is a throne, wrought from infernal iron into a serpent's likeness, unmistakably intended for Mammon. This throne, devoid of any wings to reflect Mammon's unique form, stands as a stark symbol of his power. On either side of this sinister seat, two blocks of obsidian are meticulously placed, each outfitted with chains and manacles for the grim purpose of securing slaves. Behind this daunting arrangement stands a raised stone bench, covered with a golden silk cloth, presumably serving as a place for food and drink amidst the ominous setting.
}}






This area will likely be empty unless the heroes are practicing, in which case various NPCs may be present. None of them, however, has the gall to sit in Mammon's throne. 



::


##### 4. Arena
:
{{descriptive
The arena spans 200 feet in diameter, its ground covered with 2 feet of fine, blood-red sand. At its center stands a 60-foot tall stone pillar, emerging from a 30-foot diameter stone platform that is elevated one foot above the sand. Chains and manacles hang from the pillar. Around the arena, 5 stone doors are set into the walls, seemingly without hinges or visible means of opening. The northwestern section features tiered stone seating, accommodating spectators, while to the northeast lies a viewing area dominated by a grand throne, sculpted in the likeness of a serpent-humanoid figure.
}}



\page

{{pageNumber,auto}}


### Tapestry of Mammon's Feast (combat form)




{{imageMaskEdge8,--offset:-32%,--rotation:-10
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/SsQH0NX.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->







\page

{{pageNumber,auto}}



{{descriptive
The dome's ceiling, towering 100 feet at its peak, is adorned with a series of murals that depict Minauros as an abscess festering within the swamp. These scenes possess an eerie semblance of life, with fog appearing to roll across the dome's expanse and ooze pits bubbling within the painted landscape. This dynamic portrayal is unsettling as it transforms the dome into a canvas that blurs the line between art and reality. 
}}


The sand within the arena is deliberately treated as difficult terrain, slowing the movements of the combatants to prevent them from fleeing their destiny. The chains fastened to the central pillar serve a dual purpose: they enable the chain devils to 'train' the prisoners and to inflict pain on those who resist. These chains are sufficiently long, capable of extending up to 20 feet from the pillar, allowing for a wide radius of control and punishment within the heart of the arena. 

The described doors are formidable 3-foot thick blocks of stone, designed to be opened by conventional methods solely from the outside. A mere push from the exterior triggers the doors to glide forward on rollers, smoothly swinging to one side.



::

##### 5. Tiered Steps

{{descriptive
The wide stone tiers serve as the gathering spot for crowds to witness minor contests or watch their slaves undergo training. Like much of the facility, these stones are coated in a layer of greenish oil, adding a slick sheen to the seating area. 
}}






##### 6. Comfortable Chambers

These two chambers are reserved for prominent NPCs or patrons. A male cambion named Vorenus and his pet displacer fiend will occupy one of these chambers but he only uses it as a temporary accommodation. He is described below under the Training section.

If the heroes enter one of these chambers, read or paraphrase the following: 



{{descriptive
This plush chamber exudes opulence and comfort. The room is adorned with silk cushions scattered across the floor, inviting relaxation and indulgence. Against the walls, a pair of silk cots draped in the finest weave gleam under the soft light that filters through the chamber.

Central to the chamber is a low, ornate table crafted from dark, polished wood, surrounded by an array of crystal goblets and decanters filled with what appears to be wine.

}}


The crystal-ware would fetch at least 250 gp to the right buyer.










##### 7. Mammon's Row
:
Read or paraphrase the following when the heroes approach:

{{descriptive
In this arc, four magnificent obsidian statues of Mammon stand as testament to his insatiable overindulgence, each veined with fiery orange jacinth and swamp green peridot, lending them a life-like vibrancy:

- *The Statue of Slavery:* This statue captures Mammon in a pose of dominance, one hand extended as if to command. Around his feet, miniature figures are carved, representing the slaves under his control.
:
- *The Statue of Gluttony:* Here, Mammon is depicted seated at a banquet table, his expression one of greedy delight. The table is overladen with foods, from exotic fruits to roasted beasts. His corpulent form is exaggerated, emphasizing his gluttony.
:
- *The Statue of War:* This statue portrays Mammon as a conqueror, clad in ornate armor, with raised sword. The stance is one of triumph, with fallen foes at his feet.
:
- *The Statue of Wealth:* The final statue shows Mammon seated upon a throne built of coins and treasures, a scepter of jacinth and peridot in hand. His gaze is both covetous and satisfied, surrounded by symbols of opulence.
}}







Any creature that takes an action to Investigate any of the statues must make a DC 23 Wisdom saving throw or suffer a curse that will last until the creature leaves this plane of Hell. The saving throw can be made each day at dawn but the DC increases by 1 with each failure. A natural roll of 20 is always successful. 
:
The curse corresponds to the statue being examined:

- **Slavery.** The hero is compelled to attack and slay any slaves they encounter. The Dungeon Master takes control of the hero's actions until no slaves remain that they are aware of. The Dungeon Master decides whether an NPC is a slave or not.
- **Gluttony.**  The hero is consumed by an insatiable hunger, devouring their supplies and always seeking more, to the point of illness. The lure of food can distract them, potentially impacting their focus in combat and other situations.
- **War.** The hero becomes aggressive in combat, charging forward regardless of the risk. For spellcasters, this means using their most potent spells and magical items indiscriminately, targeting even the weakest foes with their strongest magic.
- **Wealth.** Overcome with greed, the hero seeks to accumulate all visible wealth, even resorting to theft. Their obsession with gold and gemstones can distract them from immediate threats, prioritizing the acquisition of treasure over engaging in combat.





\page

{{pageNumber,auto}}



{{imageMaskEdge8,--offset:-30%,--rotation:-12
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/kD5LCJn.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->













\page

{{pageNumber,auto}}









##### 8. Champion's Row

Read or paraphrase the following when the heroes approach:



{{descriptive
In this curved hallway, eight strikingly lifelike figures are arrayed, each frozen in a dynamic combat pose. Upon closer examination, it becomes clear that these are not mere statues but the work of a master taxidermist.

From an elf with a tightly drawn bow to a towering fire giant brandishing a colossal sword, these figures capture the essence of battle. A gnome is depicted in the act of spellcasting, fingers spread in the midst of conjuration, while a kenku dashes forward, dagger at the ready. A half-elf appears to move silently with blades unsheathed, a dwarf raises a symbol of faith as if calling upon divine intervention, a half-orc brandishes an axe in a lethal arc, and a figure with an otherworldly aura focuses an invisible force.
}}



The figures are, in order of description:

1. Elara the wood elf ranger (9th level)
2. Gaius the **fire giant**
3. Thistle the gnome wizard (11th level)
4. Drax the **kenku**
5. Sylvi the half-elf rogue (13th level)
6. Borin the dwarf cleric (14th level)
7. Fen the half-orc fighter (9th level)
8. Luna the **Githzerai Zerth**

These figures, once gladiators of varying renown, met their end only to be preserved through the art of taxidermy by a necromancer. His exceptional skill has ensured that these creatures remain in excellent condition, a testament to both his craft and their former prowess.

Should the heroes find themselves in need of additional allies and possess the means to resurrect these individuals, they have the opportunity to do so. For the purposes of any resurrection attempt, it should be assumed that each creature has been deceased for a duration determined by rolling 15d10 years.

Additionally, if the heroes are currently lacking equipment or magical items, this setting offers a chance to bolster their arsenal. However, it is noted that the weapons wielded by these taxidermied gladiators are non-magical, serving more as relics of their past lives than as enhancements for the present.


:::::::::::



##### Additional security

Each entry/exit point from the facility is guarded by a trio of **spined devils** that perch atop the structure.
::


##### Training 

If the heroes are quartered in the Lesser Pit facility, their patron is likely to insist on their participation in training sessions between their main arena battles in the Sloth Pit. These exercises aim to hone the heroes' skills and endurance, possibly weeding out any under-performers in the group. If the heroes need experience to attain a level, this is an excellent opportunity. 

The heroes will have access to basic non-magical equipment as listed in the *Player's Handbook*, available without limit.

While these encounters are not meant to be lethal, the heroes are to remain unaware of this fact to maintain a sense of danger. They may feel naked fighting without their equipment, which is the intent. 

Each fight is monitored by the two **chain devils** mentioned earlier, along with a male **cambion** named Vorenus. Vorenus is never seen without his pet **displacer fiend**, detailed in *Chains of Asmodeus*, page 240. 

Escape from this facility, while possible, is unlikely.
::
##### Practice Matches

The heroes face a variety of opponents, with the timing of these bouts left to your discretion, ensuring they align with the flow of your campaign.
:
Opponents might include:

1. A trio of **chasme demons**. The demons are brought into the arena in soundproof metal cages and released at different points around the perimeter. 
:
2. A **vorvolaka** (see stat block below). Eternally craving mortal blood, this bat-like entity is bound by chains within the arena, awaiting its release. Lacking magical weapons, the heroes will find it a formidable adversary.



{{imageMaskEdge8,--offset:32%,--rotation:-12
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/mz6TXJG.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->





\page
{{pageNumber,auto}}

### A Vorvolaka



{{imageMaskEdge8,--offset:-33%,--rotation:-4
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/7mu66V1.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->















\page

{{pageNumber,auto}}


{{monster,frame
## Vorvolaka 
*Medium Undead, Neutral Evil*
___
**Armor Class** 18 (Natural Armor)  
**Hit Points** 190 (20d8 + 100)  
**Speed** 20 ft., Fly 50 ft.  

___
| STR | DEX | CON | INT | WIS | CHA |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 15 (+2) | 19 (+4) | 21 (+5) | 8 (–1) | 13 (+1) | 16 (+3) |
___
**Saving Throws** Con +10, Wis +6  
**Skills** Insight +6, Perception +11, Religion +4, Survival +6  
**Damage Resistances** bludgeoning, piercing and slashing from nonmagical attacks  
**Damage Immunities** necrotic  
**Condition Immunities** charmed, exhaustion, paralyzed  
**Senses** darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 21  
**Languages** The languages it knew in life  
**Challenge** 14 (11,500 XP) **Proficiency Bonus** +5  
___
### **Traits**  

***Fear Aura.*** Any hostile creature that starts its turn within 20 feet of the vorvolaka must succeed on a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened until the start of its next turn. If a creature succeeds on this saving throw, it is immune to the vorvolaka’s Fear Aura for the next 24 hours. This ability does not function if the vorvolaka is unconscious.

:

***Magic Resistance.*** The vorvolaka has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.  
:
***Regeneration.*** The vorvolaka regains 15 hit points at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 hit point. If the vorvolaka takes radiant damage, this trait does not function at the start of its next turn.  

### **Actions**  

***Multiattack.*** The vorvolaka makes two Talon attacks. It can replace one of these attacks with Rend Armor (if available).  
:
***Talon.*** *Melee Weapon Attack:* +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. *Hit:* 22 (4d8 + 4) piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) necrotic damage.  
:
***Rend Armor (Recharge 4–6).*** The vorvolaka makes a Talon attack against a creature. On a hit, the target’s AC is reduced by 2 for 1 minute. Creatures not wearing armor are immune to this effect, and the total reduction cannot bring a creature’s AC below 10.  
}}




::


















### Suggested Timeline
:
Matches are scheduled weekly, though the frequency can be adjusted as you see fit, ensuring the heroes have ample time for recovery and preparation between contests.


::

Between battles, and assuming they are free souls, the heroes have the liberty to roam the city and its outskirts.

As the date of the next battle draws near, anticipation mounts within the city. The heroes' growing fame catches the eyes of the city's inhabitants.

Additionally, the heroes could encounter prominent individuals keen on influencing their fate in the arena. These figures may extend offerings designed to bolster the heroes' prowess in combat, such as magical artifacts, or they might surreptitiously bestow cursed items intended to seal their doom. The specifics of these encounters and the nature of the gifts are left to the discretion of the Dungeon Master to develop and integrate into the narrative. 

This is a good opportunity to foreshadow events to come in other layers of Hell.

::

### Simenon Goldentongue
:

Simenon, a half-elf bard of the College of Lore, originates from the distant city of Neverwinter. His journey took a dark turn when he was captured in Avernus and subsequently sold to Mammon. Over the years, Simenon has become an indispensable figure in Minauros, serving Mammon as a chronicler, songwriter, spy, and occasionally, an assassin. Simenon favors a subtle and indirect approach to his darker duties, with poisoning being his method of choice. This allows him to maintain his guise as a mere bard while discreetly eliminating threats or manipulating situations to Mammon's advantage.

Initially of neutral good alignment, his prolonged exposure to Mammon's influence has shifted his morality towards neutral evil.

As Mammon's entertainer, Simenon's talents have been leveraged not just for amusement but as a tool for espionage and manipulation. Should Mammon act as the patron of the heroes, Simenon will engage with them at the Blighted Tower or their current residence, eager to immortalize their exploits in song. He becomes a conduit for Mammon, offering encouragement, intelligence on adversaries and magical support when necessary.

Conversely, if Mammon does not sponsor the heroes, Simenon's interactions are underpinned by ulterior motives. He seeks to uncover their vulnerabilities under the guise of gathering material for his compositions, intending to relay this information back to Mammon.

Renowned for his captivating performances, Simenon's presence is a staple at the arena battles, where his melodious voice and creative lyrics celebrate the victors, weaving their deeds into the annals of Minauros through his music. 




\page



:
{{pageNumber,auto}}


![cat warrior](https://i.imgur.com/5QOBZJP.png) {width:325px,mix-blend-mode:multiply}

:::
Below is an example of Simenon's ballad that will be sung if the heroes defeat Asmodeus' hero, the war devil Malthus Hellbringer. 

::

{{descriptive
*In the land where shadows reign, under a sky forever dim, 
Heroes rose, hearts aflame, to challenge fate and whim.*
:
*Against Malthus Hellbringer, terror of the Ninth layer's night, 
They stood firm, their resolve as strong as their might.*
:
*From Asmodeus' dark domain, the fiend had roared his claim,
With eyes like coals and breath of flames, he sought to spread his fame.*
:
*Yet the heroes, fearless in their quest, 
Faced the War Devil, and put his might to the test.*

:
*With every clash and fiery blast, the battle fiercely raged, 
But our heroes' spirits, by no fear were caged.* 
:
*Together they fought, their courage their guide, 
Till Malthus' roar was silenced, his fury denied.*
:
*Sing, sing of the valiant, whose lights never fade, 
Against the darkness of the Ninth, their stand was made.*
:
*In songs and stories, let their deeds soar, 
For conquering Malthus Hellbringer, in Hell's deepest core.*
}}


::::::











Simenon typically steers clear of direct confrontations with the heroes, preferring the realms of espionage and intrigue. 

However, should circumstances force him into battle, below are the spells he has ready. You will need to flesh out his statistics if you envisage him in a combat scenario with the heroes.
:
**Spellcasting.** Simenon's spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 16, +8 to hit with spell attacks):

- Cantrips (at will): *Prestidigitation, Mage Hand, Vicious Mockery*
:
- 1st level (4 slots): *Disguise Self, Healing Word, Tasha’s Hideous Laughter*
:
- 2nd level (3 slots): *Invisibility, Suggestion*
:
- 3rd level (3 slots): *Dispel Magic, Hypnotic Pattern*
:
- 4th level (3 slots): *Dimension Door, Greater Invisibility*
:
- 5th level (2 slots): *Animate Objects, Dominate Person*
:
- 6th level (1 slot): *Mass Suggestion*








::::
### Final Thoughts
:


I hope you have enjoyed this adventure and that your heroes have survived the pit, forging alliances, rivalries and hard-earned scars along the way.
:
Ideally, they should flee Minauros in haste, pursued by Mammon’s relentless death squads, their next adventure waiting just beyond  . . . 

::

Christopher Hamwell.

























\page

{{backCover}}

# The Sloth Pit of Minauros
:
Welcome to the Sloth Pit—where only blood buys freedom ...
:
Deep in the filthy heart of Minauros, where the swamps swallow the weak and the gold of Mammon stains every deal, stands the Sloth Pit—the deadliest arena in the Nine Hells. Here, chains or coin decide your fate. Are you a slave, fighting for the amusement of your master? A gladiator, spilling blood for the glory of your patron—perhaps Mammon, or even Asmodeus himself?
:
It doesn’t matter.
:
Only the strong survive. Only the ruthless see another round.
:
The crowds roar for carnage. The pitmasters demand entertainment. And you? You must kill or be killed. Because in the Sloth Pit, there’s only one rule:
:
Fight . . . or die.
___

arguments.content:
{{pageNumber,auto}}



{{frontCover}}



# El Pozo de la Pereza de Minauros
## Ampliando Chains of Asmodeus
___

{{banner Christopher Hamwell}}

{{footnote
  *Para sobrevivir, tendrás que matar . . .* 
}}

![background image](https://i.imgur.com/FHoNkl8.png){position:absolute,bottom:0,left:0,height:100%}

\page


{{pageNumber,auto}}




# El Pozo de la Pereza de Minauros



Para sobrevivir, debes matar...

:


Bienvenido a Minauros, un reino donde la propia esencia de la desolación toma forma. El aire que respiras está contaminado de veneno, una amenaza invisible que se filtra en tus pulmones, llenando cada aliento con el sabor de la corrupción. La descomposición está en todas partes, testimonio del ciclo implacable de destrucción y renacimiento que define esta capa del Infierno.
:
Este pantano interminable es un paisaje de desesperación. La visibilidad se reduce a apenas unos pocos pies por delante, y lo que yace más allá queda a la imaginación o, quizá, a las pesadillas de quienes se atreven a aventurarse aquí. Los restos de antiguas estructuras sobresalen del lodo como dedos esqueléticos que acusan al cielo, mientras el goteo constante de la humedad compone una cacofonía que llena el aire de fatalidad.

La flora y la fauna de Minauros se han adaptado a este infierno adquiriendo formas retorcidas. La vegetación es escasa y se limita sobre todo a árboles nudosos que rezuman una savia tan negra como la noche y a arbustos espinosos que desgarran la carne con un hambre nacida del propio pantano. Las criaturas que habitan este lugar no son menos grotescas, desde enjambres de insectos que zumban sin descanso alrededor de la materia en descomposición hasta depredadores mayores que se mueven en silencio a través de la niebla.

Navegar por Minauros es un desafío no solo de resistencia física, sino de fortaleza mental. El pantano juega trucos con la mente, con senderos que cambian sin que uno se dé cuenta y susurros que resuenan desde ninguna parte. La ciudad de Minauros misma se alza como un monumento a la codicia y la desesperación; sus edificios se hunden lentamente en el fango y, aun así, sus habitantes siguen conspirando e intriganando, con ambiciones tan ilimitadas como el pantano que los aprisiona.

Solo los más audaces o los más desesperados hacen de este lugar su hogar. Bienvenido a Minauros, donde cada paso es una batalla contra la putrefacción que avanza, y cada aliento una muestra de desafío al infierno que busca reclamarte.
::
### ¿Qué es esto?
:
Esta aventura está diseñada para héroes de nivel 13 y se centra en una arena gladiatoria llamada el Pozo de la Pereza, en la ciudad de Minauros. 

Antes que nada, debes decidir si tus jugadores actúan por voluntad propia o si son cautivos. En estas páginas se describen ambos escenarios para que los explores.  

Recuerda que esta aventura no está pensada para ser estrictamente lineal. Como Dungeon Master, deberías leer el módulo completo antes de dirigirlo para asegurar una experiencia fluida.
::
##### 1. Atrapados en el Infierno
Tus aventureros pueden haber sido capturados lejos de los pantanos de Minauros, solo para descubrirse en las garras de algún poderoso habitante del Infierno. Se convierten en combatientes involuntarios del Pozo de la Pereza, una arena infame en el corazón de Minauros. Para recuperar su libertad, deberán imponerse a equipos rivales, ganando no solo su libertad, sino también el favor y el aplauso de su captor.
::
##### 2. Mercenarios de la Arena
Surge una oportunidad cuando una figura distinguida de la ciudad extiende una invitación a tus héroes: *"Pon tu poder al servicio de mi causa, y báñate en el botín de la victoria y en el resplandor de la fama."* Aquí, la decisión de entrar en la refriega es exclusivamente suya, impulsada por la atracción de la riqueza y el renombre. El caótico Tiax es un ejemplo, pero hay muchos otros, incluyendo al propio Asmodeus.



Sea cual sea el camino que tomes, tus héroes necesitarán un patrón y un nombre que infunda temor en el corazón de sus adversarios.

Si a tus héroes les cuesta encontrar un nombre apropiado, su patrón elegirá uno por ellos. Aquí tienes algunas sugerencias:
- La Venganza Carmesí
- La Vanguardia Fantasma
- La Garra Negra
- Hojas de Sombra
::
### Patrones
:
Sea cual sea el camino elegido, conseguir un patrón es crucial. El patrón es el dueño del equipo que compite en los Pozos de la Pereza. Como *Chains of Asmodeus* ofrece muchos PNJ formidables, las opciones son abundantes. Aunque no todos los patrones participan directamente en los asuntos cotidianos, cabe esperar que hagan acto de presencia en los combates, ya sea para alabar o criticar a sus luchadores, o para ofrecer apoyo mágico. A continuación se presentan varias sugerencias de patrones: 

:
##### Asmodeus 
Sí, el propio Asmodeus se interesa por los Pozos de la Pereza, buscando obtener derechos de fanfarronería sobre sus parientes inferiores. Si ese es el caso, los héroes serán marcados con una serpiente negra en el pecho para indicar que le pertenecen. 



{{imageMaskEdge6,--offset:45%,--rotation:-12
  ![](https://i.imgur.com/YSOW7lX.png){height:100%}
}}
<!-- Use --offset to shift the mask away from page center (can use cm instead of %)
     Use --rotation to set rotation angle in degrees. -->




\page
{{pageNumber,auto}}






##### Tiax
Escoger a Tiax encaja especialmente bien para aventureros que no hayan sido llevados por la fuerza. Este clérigo de Cyric no solo sirve a su deidad; también posee un equipo gladiatorio que compite en los Pozos de la Pereza. Tiax es infame por enredar a héroes incautos. Aliarse con él introduce a los aventureros en un mundo de caos, donde cada decisión podría formar parte de una intriga aún más compleja. 
:
##### Klassk (dragón del Estigia)
Klassk, un dragón del Estigia normalmente encontrado en el Ineffable Trove, entra en escena como patrón de equipo. Podría haberse cruzado con los héroes en el Río Estigia, donde disfruta emboscando viajeros. El encuentro puede conducir a un trato si ataca su barcaza o si los héroes se adentran en su dominio. Los principales motivos de Klassk son la búsqueda de poder y tesoros, y ve a los héroes como posibles llaves para alcanzar esos deseos. 
:
##### Mammon 
El soberano de Minauros, cuya pasión por la riqueza no conoce límites, es un patrón inigualable para gladiadores en la arena de la ciudad. Como gobernante del mismo dominio que alberga los fosos de combate, su implicación aporta un prestigio e influencia sin parangón. Con un ojo siempre puesto en acumular riquezas, este patrón ve a los héroes como valiosos activos en su búsqueda de prosperidad.
:

##### Otros
Considera cualquier PNJ poderoso que los héroes hayan encontrado a lo largo de sus aventuras. También puedes anticipar PNJ que aparecerán en capas más profundas del Infierno.

::




### El Anunciador de la Arena
:
Voxskar, el anunciador goblin de la arena, es una figura querida en el foso. Cubierto con una capa sobredimensionada confeccionada con pieles de gibbering mouthers, ofrece una figura cómica con su rostro aniñado y su pequeña complexión. La capa le concede el poder de amplificar su voz (ver más abajo), asegurando que nadie se pierda sus absurdos comentarios.

Entretenedor nato, Voxskar establece rápidamente vínculos con los combatientes de la arena, otorgando a cada uno apodos extravagantes y humorísticos. Su entusiasmo por el espectáculo solo es superado por su amor por el suministro interminable de ashskitters ensartados, pequeños lagartos chamuscados nativos de Minauros. Siempre ingenioso, usa a menudo estos tentempiés como punteros improvisados mientras narra las luchas.

A pesar de su carácter juguetón, Voxskar es apreciado por los habitantes de la arena y está ferozmente protegido frente a cualquier amenaza.

Voxskar lleva *Boots of Levitation*, lo que le permite levitar sobre la arena mientras narra las carnicerías que se desarrollan abajo. Sus comentarios, tan vívidos como sangriento es el combate, aportan un toque único al espectáculo. 
::
Es conocido por frases como *"Parece que ese luchador acaba de recibir un corte de pelo gratis . . . ¡lástima que haya sido desde el cuello hacia arriba!"* y *"¡No veía a nadie girar así desde la última vez que bebí sangre de dragón fermentada!"* 



:

Entre combate y combate, se acerca a los héroes y los abruma con una interminable catarata de preguntas con las que enriquecer su narración. Sus preguntas suelen parecer completamente inútiles, pero las formula con tal entusiasmo infantil que resulta difícil ignorarlo.

- *“¿Cuál es tu comida favorita del Infierno? No

# El Pozo de la Pereza de Minauros



Para sobrevivir, debes matar...

:

La descomposición está en todas partes, testimonio del ciclo implacable de destrucción y renacimiento que define esta capa del Infierno.
:

La visibilidad se reduce a apenas unos pocos pies por delante, y lo que yace más allá queda a la imaginación o, quizá, a las pesadillas de quienes se atreven a aventurarse aquí.
